full_text:
Sample had a tumor nodule with some surrounding adjacent tissue, which was removed.

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H).

Dispase dissociation media included 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37 C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++, 1 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 100 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37 C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 4 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for both conditions.

Dispase dissociated cells: The final pellet was resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12+++ which was later replaced with iPDC media.

Sequentially dissociated cells: The final pellet was resuspended in 2 of DMEM/F12+++.

For counting, a 1:10 dilution of both cell suspensions was made.

Cell counts:

Aliquots for flow cytometry:

6x 1.5 E6 cells of dispase dissociated cells

1x 1.5 E6 cells of sequentially dissociated cells

For enrichment

took 3x 60 E6 cells of sequential dissociated cells

Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 4x 5 E6 cells

Sequentially dissociated cells: 2x 5 E6 cells

Sequentially dissociated cells, negative isolation flowthrough: 3x 1 E6 cells

Labelled: S543_pLN 1 E6 cells D+C&H Fib+End 02.04.2025 AE

Sequentially dissociated cells, negative isolation column bound: 4x 5 E6 cells

Labelled: S543_pLN 5 E6 cells D+C&H Imm+Can 02.04.2025 AE

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)

Performed negative isolation for all sequentially dissociated cells. Took 3x 60 E6 cells for 3 separate fractions.

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before being used.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 1500 rpm for 5 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 260 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation: the desired volume [500 µl] - the volume of both beads [2x 120 µl]). Added 120 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g) for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

After this, CD45 (TIL) microbeads cat no. 130-118-780 lot 5241203993 vial was used up.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the cell suspensions onto the columns, and washed them with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged at 1500 rpm g for 5 min at +4 C. Resuspended the flow-through fraction in 1 ml iPDC media and the column-bound fraction in 3 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Made a 1:10 dilution of column-bound fraction for counting. Counted the cells.

NOTE! These cell counts don’t make sense. The enrichment started with a total of 180 E6 cells, so there can’t be over 200 E6 cells at the end. The flow-through cell count aligns with the appearance of the pellet, but the column-bound fraction cannot be that large. Of course, the initial cell count of the sequentially dissociated cells could also be inaccurate.

The cell count in the flow-through fraction was too low to combine it with the dispase-dissociated cells and achieve a sufficient total cell population for plating and flow cytometry. Therefore, it was decided to plate the dispase-dissociated cells instead.

Cell culture

1 000 live cells/uL * 40 uL = 40 000 cells/droplet

40 000 cells/droplet * 8 droplets/well * 25 wells (one extra) = 8 000 000 live cells are needed

8 droplets/ well * 25 wells * 40 uL/ droplet = 8 000 uL are needed in total

However, since the omentum gel start polymerizing as soon as the fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make sixteen (16) separate tubes each one of 500 uL (8000 uL/16 and not everything at once in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/uL, so the total number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 live cells/uL * 500 uL = 0.50 E6 live cells/tube

Note: Cells were re-counted before plating and the average count was 78 E6 cells in 1.7 mL = 45.8 E6 cells/mL. When average viability (58.5%) was taken into account: 45.8 E6 cells/ mL * 0.585 = 26.8 E6 live cells/mL. However, there was a mistake because 78 E6 cells was not the total number of cells, but the density, so when accounting for the viability it was 45.63 E6 live cells/mL. Meaning that cells were plated at 1.7x the desired density. Therefore, this culture will be more confluent that others.

0.50 E6 cells / (26.8 E6 cells/mL) = 18.7 uL from the original cell suspension * 16 = 299.2 uL

The amount of the remaining components per tube will be:

ORG full medium: (500 uL – 142.2 uL) = 357.8 uL * 16 = 5724.8 uL == 5725 uL

Omentum gel: 110.5 uL * 16 = 1768 uL (884 uL * 2)

Aprotinin: 1.5 * 16 = 24 uL

Thrombin: 1.5 * 16 = 24 uL

Fibrinogen: 10

Total (ome-gel+apro+thro+fib) = 123.5 uL

Total (+cells) = 142.2 uL

Note: The sixteen tubes were not prepared individually, but all the ingredientes except the fibrinogen where mixed in a 15 mL Falcon tube (calculated value/tube * 16) and then dispensed into the 16 eppis. After that, the fibrinogen was added individually. However, The mix was only enough for 14 eppis, not sixteen, and because of these only three and a half plates were plated.

The droplets were allowed to gelify for 1 hour in the incubator and, after that time, 3.5 mL of iPDC media + cytokines were added into each well. Pictures were taken in the cell culture microscope under all the magnifications available.

Flow cytometry

The conditions to stain were:

CD56 FMO

CD14 FMO

HLA-Dr FMO

DCM FMO

CD11c FMO

dispase dissociated cells, MC

sequentially dissociated cells, MC

The aliquots were stained with the following panel.

Washed cells once with 900 µl FACS buffer (centrifuge 6min 400 g at +4°C).

The antibodies were diluted in Brilliant stain buffer (#563794 BD Horizon), 50 µl of the antibody mix was added to each sample, and the samples were incubated for 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

For the FMO samples, antibody mixes were prepared separately

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Added 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) to fix the cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

Next morning, centrifuged and resuspended the cells in 400 µl FACS buffer.

Pipeted the cell suspension through a 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Preparation of compensation controls

Used BD CompBeads compensation beads (small, not Plus ones).

Added one drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG, #51-90-9001229) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-90-90001291) compensation beads to 5ml Falcon polystyrene round bottom tubes (white cap).

For stained compensation controls, 1 µl of each antibody was used.

the same antibodies except CD16-BV510 instead of BV510-DCM.

Incubated for 30 min at +4°C shielded from light and resuspended to 400 µl FACS buffer.
